lessons.title: Stacked Form
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to design a vertically stacked form using HTML and CSS. By structuring form inputs and labels sequentially and applying specific CSS styling, you can create a clean, responsive layout where elements are positioned on top of each other for improved readability.

lessons.title: Responsive Form
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to build a responsive form using HTML and CSS. It explains how to structure form elements with containers and apply media queries to ensure labels and inputs stack vertically on smaller screens, providing an optimal user experience across different devices.

lessons.title: Popup Form
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating an interactive popup form using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the essential steps for structuring the form, applying necessary styles for positioning and visibility, and implementing basic functions to open and close the interface.

lessons.title: Inline Form
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to creating responsive inline forms using HTML and CSS. By utilizing Flexbox and media queries, you will learn how to arrange form elements horizontally on larger screens while ensuring they stack vertically for improved usability on smaller devices.

lessons.title: Clear Input Field
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates two methods for clearing HTML input fields: automatically upon focus using the `onfocus` attribute and manually via a button click using JavaScript. These techniques provide simple, efficient ways to manage user input and improve form interactivity.

lessons.title: Hide Number Arrows
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on how to remove default increment/decrement spinners from HTML number inputs using CSS. By applying specific browser-targeted styles, developers can create a cleaner interface while maintaining the input's underlying functionality.

lessons.title: Copy Text to Clipboard
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a "copy to clipboard" feature using HTML and JavaScript. It covers selecting input field text, utilizing the Clipboard API for copying, and enhancing the user experience by adding a CSS-based tooltip for visual feedback.

lessons.title: Animated Search
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create an interactive, animated search form using HTML and CSS. By applying a CSS transition to the input field's width property, the form expands smoothly when clicked, providing a polished and responsive user experience.

lessons.title: Search Button
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating a functional search button using HTML and CSS. It covers the necessary markup for a search form and demonstrates how to apply styling, including icon integration and responsive layout techniques, to achieve a professional-looking interface.

lessons.title: Fullscreen Search
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a responsive fullscreen search overlay using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the structural setup, styling for the overlay effect, and the necessary functions to toggle the search box visibility for an improved user experience.

lessons.title: Input Field in Navbar
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to integrate a search input field and submit button into a navigation menu. It provides the necessary HTML structure and CSS styling to ensure the navbar remains functional and responsive across different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Login Form in Navbar
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on integrating a login form directly into a website's navigation menu. It covers the necessary HTML structure and CSS styling required to create a functional, responsive layout that adapts seamlessly to various screen sizes.

lessons.title: Custom Checkbox/Radio
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating custom checkboxes and radio buttons using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to hide default browser inputs and use styled span elements to achieve a unique, consistent design across different web interfaces.

lessons.title: Custom Select
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating custom select boxes using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It demonstrates how to hide the default browser element and replace it with a styled, interactive component that functions through custom event handling.

lessons.title: Toggle Switch
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to creating functional toggle switches using HTML and CSS. By utilizing a hidden checkbox and styled slider elements, learners can implement both rectangular and rounded on/off buttons with smooth transition effects.

lessons.title: Check Checkbox
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to toggle the visibility of text based on a checkbox state using JavaScript. By utilizing the `checked` property and an `onclick` event listener, you can dynamically update the CSS `display` style of an HTML element to show or hide content interactively.

lessons.title: Detect Caps Lock
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to detect if the Caps Lock key is active within an input field using JavaScript. By utilizing the `getModifierState("CapsLock")` method within a keyup event listener, developers can dynamically display or hide warning messages to improve user experience.

lessons.title: Trigger Button on Enter
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to trigger a button click using the JavaScript `keypress` event listener. By detecting the "Enter" key within an input field, you can programmatically execute a button's click action, enhancing user interaction and form accessibility.

lessons.title: Password Validation
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to build an interactive password validation form using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers implementing regex-based input restrictions and providing real-time visual feedback to users as they meet specific security requirements.

lessons.title: Toggle Password Visibility
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a password visibility toggle using HTML and JavaScript. By manipulating the input field's type attribute through a checkbox event, users can easily switch between masked and plain text views to improve their login experience.

lessons.title: Multiple Step Form
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a multi-step registration form using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It demonstrates how to manage form navigation, implement input validation, and display visual progress indicators to improve the user experience.

lessons.title: Autocomplete
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a step-by-step guide to building a custom autocomplete feature for web forms. It covers the necessary HTML structure, CSS styling for the dropdown interface, and JavaScript logic to filter data from an array and handle keyboard navigation.

lessons.title: Turn off autocomplete
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to disable browser-based text suggestions for web forms using the `autocomplete` attribute. By applying this attribute to specific input fields or entire form elements, developers can effectively prevent the browser from storing or suggesting user data.

lessons.title: Turn off spellcheck
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to disable browser-based spellcheck in HTML forms using the `spellcheck="false"` attribute. You can apply this setting to individual input fields, textareas, or an entire form element to prevent the display of red underlines for spelling errors.

lessons.title: File Upload Button
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a file upload feature using the HTML `<input type="file">` element. It provides a concise code example showing how to integrate this button within a form to enable users to select and submit files to a server-side destination.

lessons.title: Empty Input Validation
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement basic form validation using JavaScript to ensure required fields are not submitted empty. By checking the input value within a validation function, you can trigger an alert and prevent form submission if the field remains blank.

lessons.title: Filter List
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating a dynamic search filter for HTML lists using JavaScript. By combining basic HTML structure, CSS styling, and a simple JavaScript function, users learn how to filter list items in real-time based on user input.

lessons.title: Filter Table
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on implementing a real-time search filter for HTML tables using JavaScript. By combining structured HTML, CSS styling, and a dynamic filtering function, learners can enable users to quickly locate specific data entries within a table.

lessons.title: Filter Elements
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create a dynamic filtering system for web elements using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. By assigning specific classes to DIV elements, users can toggle their visibility via control buttons, effectively categorizing and displaying content based on user selection.

lessons.title: Filter Dropdown
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create a functional, searchable dropdown menu using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers structuring the dropdown container, styling the search input and menu items, and implementing a filter function to dynamically hide or show links based on user input.

lessons.title: Sort List
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on using JavaScript to sort HTML list items alphabetically or numerically. It demonstrates how to implement custom sorting functions that allow users to toggle between ascending and descending orders by manipulating DOM elements.

lessons.title: Sort Table
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement dynamic table sorting using JavaScript. It covers basic alphabetical sorting and advanced functionality, such as toggling between ascending and descending order via header clicks and handling numerical data types.

lessons.title: Zebra Striped Table
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to enhance table readability by applying alternating background colors to rows. By utilizing the CSS `nth-child()` selector with the "even" or "odd" argument, developers can easily implement a zebra-striped design for better visual organization of tabular data.

lessons.title: Center Tables
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to horizontally center an HTML table by applying CSS. By setting the left and right margins to "auto" on the table element, you can achieve alignment, provided the table width is not set to 100%.

lessons.title: Full-width Table
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to expand an HTML table to span the entire width of its container using CSS. By applying the `width: 100%` property to the table element, you can ensure a responsive layout that adapts to different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Nested Table
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create a nested table by placing one table structure inside the cell of another. It provides a clear code example demonstrating the syntax required to embed data grids within existing table rows, offering a practical approach for complex HTML layout organization.

lessons.title: Side-by-side Tables
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to display tables side-by-side using CSS float or flexbox properties. It also provides a responsive design approach by utilizing media queries to stack tables vertically on smaller screens, ensuring optimal layout across different devices.

lessons.title: Responsive Tables
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create responsive tables that remain accessible on smaller screens. By wrapping a standard table in a container element with the CSS property `overflow-x:auto`, you enable a horizontal scroll bar, ensuring content remains readable regardless of the device width.

lessons.title: Comparison Table
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building a responsive comparison table using HTML and CSS. It covers the essential structure for data organization and demonstrates how to apply custom styling, including zebra-striped rows and Font Awesome icons, to enhance visual clarity and presentation.

lessons.title: Fullscreen Video
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create a responsive, full-screen video background using CSS to cover the entire browser window. It also provides instructions for integrating HTML5 video elements and optional JavaScript functionality to include overlay content and a play/pause toggle button.

lessons.title: Modal Boxes
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building interactive modal boxes using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the essential steps for structure, styling, and functionality, including how to implement headers, footers, and animations for a professional user interface.

lessons.title: Delete Modal
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating a delete confirmation modal using HTML and CSS. It covers the necessary structural markup, styling for the modal interface, and optional JavaScript to enhance user interaction by allowing the modal to close when clicking outside its content.

lessons.title: Timeline
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building a responsive timeline using HTML and CSS. It covers the necessary structural markup and styling techniques, including the use of media queries to ensure the layout adapts seamlessly to smaller screen sizes.

lessons.title: Scroll Indicator
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to build a dynamic scroll indicator that tracks a user's progress down a webpage. By combining HTML structure, fixed CSS styling, and a JavaScript scroll event listener, you can create a visual progress bar that updates in real-time as the page is scrolled.

lessons.title: Progress Bars
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building and animating a progress bar using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the fundamental implementation of dynamic width adjustments and demonstrates how to incorporate text labels to display real-time completion percentages.

lessons.title: Skill Bar
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating visual skill bars for resumes using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure the containers and apply specific styling to represent proficiency levels through color-coded, width-adjusted progress bars.

lessons.title: Range Sliders
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building and customizing range sliders using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers essential techniques for styling slider tracks and handles, including how to implement dynamic value displays and custom visual designs like rounded shapes or icons.

lessons.title: Color Picker
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement a color selection tool in HTML using the `<input type="color">` element. It covers the basic syntax, the requirement for using hexadecimal values for color definitions, and specifies that the default color is black if no value is provided.

lessons.title: Email Field
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement an email input field in HTML using the `<input type="email">` attribute. This element automatically validates user input to ensure that the provided text follows a correct email address format.

lessons.title: Tooltips
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating interactive tooltips using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure the container and apply styling—including positioning, visibility, and transition effects—to display informative text upon hovering over a specific element.

lessons.title: Display Element Hover
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to reveal hidden content when a user hovers over a specific element. By utilizing the CSS `display` property and the adjacent sibling selector (`+`), you can effectively toggle the visibility of elements immediately following a hovered container.

lessons.title: Popups
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building interactive popups using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the essential steps for structuring the container, styling the visual elements with animations, and implementing a toggle function to control visibility upon user interaction.

lessons.title: Collapsible
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a comprehensive guide on creating interactive collapsible sections using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the implementation of basic toggle functionality, advanced animated slide-down effects, and the addition of visual icons to improve user interface clarity.

lessons.title: Calendar
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building a functional calendar interface using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure the month header, weekday labels, and date grid, while using CSS to style the layout and highlight the current day.

lessons.title: HTML Includes
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to modularize web pages by including external HTML snippets. By using a custom "w3-include-html" attribute and a custom JavaScript function, developers can dynamically fetch and inject multiple HTML files into a single document via XMLHttpRequest.

lessons.title: To Do List
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building an interactive to-do list using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers essential techniques for structuring the interface, styling elements for better usability, and implementing functional logic to add, check off, and remove tasks dynamically.

lessons.title: Loaders
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create a CSS-based preloader using HTML containers and keyframe animations. It covers styling properties like border-radius and width to define the shape, while demonstrating how to implement continuous rotation and customize the spinner with multiple colors.

lessons.title: Badges
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create visual badges using HTML and CSS to highlight additional information. By applying a span element with specific styling properties like background color, padding, and border-radius, you can easily integrate functional labels into your web content.

lessons.title: Star Rating
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a star rating component using CSS and the Font Awesome icon library. By combining HTML span elements with a simple CSS class to control color, you can easily create a visual rating system for your web projects.

lessons.title: User Rating
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide for building a responsive user rating scorecard using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to integrate icon libraries for star ratings and use CSS layout techniques to create visual progress bars that represent review distributions.

lessons.title: Overlay Effect
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a full-page overlay effect using a combination of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. By utilizing fixed positioning and z-index, you can create a semi-transparent layer that toggles visibility to display content or messages over your existing web page.

lessons.title: Contact Chips
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to design contact chips using HTML and CSS, focusing on styling elements with images and rounded borders. It also provides a practical guide for implementing a functional close button that allows users to hide the chip via a simple JavaScript onclick event.

lessons.title: Cards
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating visually appealing UI cards using HTML and CSS. It covers essential techniques such as implementing box shadows for depth, adding hover effects for interactivity, and styling elements with rounded corners to enhance modern web design layouts.

lessons.title: Flip Card
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating an interactive 3D flip card using HTML and CSS. By utilizing CSS transitions and the transform property, learners can implement a hover-triggered animation that reveals the back side of a card element.

lessons.title: Profile Card
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building a professional profile card using HTML and CSS. It covers the essential steps for structuring the card layout, styling elements like buttons and icons, and implementing hover effects to create an interactive user interface.

lessons.title: Product Card
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building a responsive product card using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure the card elements and apply styling, including box shadows, typography, and interactive button effects, to create a professional e-commerce display.

lessons.title: Alerts
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create customizable alert messages using HTML and CSS to notify users of status changes. It covers styling alert boxes, implementing a close button functionality, and using JavaScript to manage multiple alerts with smooth fade-out transition effects.

lessons.title: Callout
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create a fixed-position callout message using HTML and CSS to notify users of tips or important updates. It provides a step-by-step guide on structuring the container and implementing a functional close button to dismiss the notification.

lessons.title: Notes
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create visual "notes" using CSS to highlight specific information such as warnings, success messages, or danger alerts. By combining semantic HTML div elements with custom CSS styling, users can effectively draw attention to important content on their websites.

lessons.title: Labels
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create and style visual labels using HTML and CSS. By applying specific classes to span elements, you can implement color-coded indicators for different categories such as success, information, warnings, and danger.

lessons.title: Ribbon
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create a decorative CSS ribbon for UI elements. By combining HTML span tags with absolute positioning and CSS rotation, you can effectively highlight specific content like "NEW" or "HOT" labels on buttons to enhance visual engagement.

lessons.title: Tag Cloud
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating a visually appealing tag cloud using HTML and CSS. By applying specific styling properties like padding, border-radius, and background colors to span elements, you can effectively organize and display a list of tags on your website.

lessons.title: Circles
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create circular elements using CSS. By setting equal height and width values and applying a 50% border-radius to an HTML span element, you can effectively transform a square container into a perfect circle.

lessons.title: Style HR
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to customize the appearance of the HTML `<hr>` element using CSS. It demonstrates how to modify horizontal rules by applying various border properties, including styles for thickness, color, dash patterns, and rounded corners.

lessons.title: Coupon
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on designing a digital coupon using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure the layout with containers and apply specific styling properties, such as dotted borders and rounded corners, to create a professional-looking promotional element.

lessons.title: List Group
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to transform a standard HTML list into a visually structured "list group" using CSS. By removing default list styling and applying custom borders, padding, and background colors, you can create a clean, professional interface for displaying grouped content.

lessons.title: List Group with Badges
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to transform a standard HTML list into a styled list group using CSS. By applying specific border, padding, and background properties, you can create a professional interface that incorporates circular badges to display numerical data effectively.

lessons.title: List Without Bullets
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to remove default bullet points from an unordered list using the CSS `list-style-type: none` property. It further instructs developers to reset the `margin` and `padding` to zero to ensure the list is properly aligned and free of default browser spacing.

lessons.title: Responsive Text
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement responsive typography using CSS. It covers scaling text dynamically based on the browser's viewport width using the "vw" unit and adjusting font sizes for specific screen dimensions through CSS media queries.

lessons.title: Cutout Text
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create responsive cutout text using CSS. By utilizing the `mix-blend-mode` property, developers can make text appear transparent against a background image. Note that this technique is not supported in Internet Explorer or Edge.

lessons.title: Glowing Text
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create a neon glowing text effect using CSS. By combining the `text-shadow` property with `keyframes` and `animation`, you can produce a dynamic, pulsating light effect that repeats infinitely.

lessons.title: Fixed Footer
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a fixed, sticky footer using CSS. By applying the "position: fixed" property along with bottom and width constraints, you can ensure the footer remains anchored to the bottom of the browser window regardless of page content.

lessons.title: Sticky Element
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement a sticky element using the CSS `position: sticky` property. It details how elements toggle between relative and fixed positioning based on scroll offsets, noting that a directional constraint like `top` is required for the effect to function correctly.

lessons.title: Equal Height
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create equal-height columns using CSS table properties or the modern Flexbox layout. It also provides guidance on implementing responsive design by using media queries to stack columns vertically on smaller screens.

lessons.title: Clearfix
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to resolve layout issues caused by floating elements overflowing their containers. It introduces the "clearfix" hack, comparing the simple `overflow: auto` method with the more robust, modern pseudo-element approach using `::after` to ensure proper layout containment.

lessons.title: Responsive Floats
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement responsive floating elements using CSS media queries. By defining specific pixel breakpoints, you can control whether an element floats based on the user's screen width, ensuring flexible layouts across different devices.

lessons.title: Snackbar
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to creating a snackbar notification using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. You will learn how to style the element for fixed positioning, implement fade-in and fade-out animations, and use a JavaScript function to trigger the display for a set duration.

lessons.title: Fullscreen Window
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement fullscreen functionality using the JavaScript `requestFullscreen()` method. It covers both element-specific and full-page modes, including cross-browser compatibility prefixes and CSS styling techniques for fullscreen states.

lessons.title: Scroll Drawing
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create an interactive scroll-drawing effect using JavaScript and SVG path elements. By calculating the scroll percentage and manipulating the stroke-dashoffset property, you can dynamically animate the drawing of shapes as the user scrolls down the page.

lessons.title: Smooth Scroll
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement smooth scrolling using the CSS `scroll-behavior: smooth` property. It also provides a cross-browser fallback solution using jQuery for environments where native CSS support is unavailable.

lessons.title: Gradient Bg Scroll
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a linear gradient background that spans the entire length of a scrollable webpage. By applying a CSS linear-gradient property to the body element with an extended height, you can create a dynamic, multi-colored background effect for your site.

lessons.title: Sticky Header
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create a sticky header that remains fixed at the top of the viewport during scrolling. By applying the CSS `position: sticky` property along with a specified offset, you can easily ensure your navigation elements stay visible as users navigate your webpage.

lessons.title: Shrink Header on Scroll
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to creating a dynamic header that shrinks when the user scrolls down the page. By combining CSS transitions for smooth visual effects with JavaScript to detect scroll position, you can effectively optimize screen space for a better user experience.

lessons.title: Pricing Table
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a responsive pricing table using HTML and CSS. You will learn how to structure data into columns and apply styling, including hover effects and media queries, to ensure the layout adapts seamlessly to different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Parallax
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement a parallax scrolling effect using CSS by applying the `background-attachment: fixed` property to a container. It also provides guidance on scaling images for full-screen layouts and using media queries to disable the effect on mobile devices.

lessons.title: Aspect Ratio
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to maintain consistent element proportions using CSS. It covers the traditional method of using percentage-based padding to preserve aspect ratios and introduces the modern `aspect-ratio` property for simpler, more efficient responsive design.

lessons.title: Responsive Iframes
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create responsive iframes that maintain a consistent aspect ratio during resizing. By wrapping an iframe in a container and using CSS padding-top percentages, developers can ensure embedded content scales proportionally across different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Toggle Like/Dislike
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create an interactive like/dislike toggle button using HTML, Font Awesome icons, and JavaScript. By utilizing the classList.toggle method, users can seamlessly switch between thumbs-up and thumbs-down icons with a single click.

lessons.title: Toggle Hide/Show
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create a toggle effect for HTML elements using JavaScript. By manipulating the CSS display property through a button click, you can easily switch an element between visible and hidden states.

lessons.title: Toggle Dark Mode
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a dark mode toggle using CSS and JavaScript. By defining a specific CSS class and utilizing the JavaScript `classList.toggle` method, developers can easily switch between light and dark themes on a webpage.

lessons.title: Toggle Text
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to toggle text content within an HTML element using JavaScript. By implementing a simple function triggered by a button click, users learn to swap between two distinct text strings by checking and updating the element's innerHTML property.

lessons.title: Toggle Class
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to dynamically switch a CSS class on an HTML element using the JavaScript `classList.toggle()` method. By combining a button trigger with this property, users can efficiently add or remove styles to create interactive web page elements.

lessons.title: Add Class
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to dynamically add a CSS class to an HTML element using JavaScript. By utilizing the `classList.add()` method, you can trigger style changes on specific elements through user interactions like button clicks.

lessons.title: Remove Class
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to remove a specific CSS class from an HTML element using JavaScript. By utilizing the `classList.remove()` method, you can dynamically update an element's styling triggered by user interactions like button clicks.

lessons.title: Change Class
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to modify an element's class name using JavaScript's `classList` property. By utilizing the `remove()` and `add()` methods, you can effectively update the styling or behavior of a specific DOM element dynamically.

lessons.title: Active Class
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to dynamically highlight a selected element by toggling an "active" CSS class using JavaScript. It provides a practical approach to managing button states by looping through elements, removing the class from the previous selection, and applying it to the current one.

lessons.title: Tree View
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building an interactive, hierarchical tree view using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It demonstrates how to structure nested lists and implement toggle functionality, allowing users to expand or collapse branches through clickable icons.

lessons.title: Remove Decimals
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to remove the decimal portion of a number in JavaScript using the `Math.trunc()` method. By applying this function, you can easily truncate floating-point values to return only the integer part.

lessons.title: Remove Property
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to use the JavaScript `delete` operator to remove both the value and the key from an object. It emphasizes that this operator is strictly for object properties and warns against using it on predefined JavaScript properties to avoid potential application crashes.

lessons.title: Offline Detection
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to determine a browser's connectivity status using the `navigator.onLine` property in JavaScript. By accessing this Boolean value, developers can easily detect whether a user is currently online or offline to improve application responsiveness.

lessons.title: Find Hidden Element
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to detect if a web element is hidden using JavaScript's `getComputedStyle` method. It explains the functional differences between `display: none`, which removes an element from the layout, and `visibility: hidden`, which hides the element while preserving its space.

lessons.title: Redirect Webpage
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to redirect users to a new URL using JavaScript's `location.href` and `location.replace` methods. It highlights the key distinction that `replace()` removes the current page from the browser history, preventing users from navigating back to the original document.

lessons.title: Format a Number
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to use the JavaScript `toFixed()` method to format numbers to a specific number of decimal places. It demonstrates that the method automatically rounds the value to the desired precision, providing a simple way to control numeric output in your code.

lessons.title: Zoom Hover
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create a zoom effect on web elements using CSS. By applying the `transform: scale()` property combined with a `transition` for smooth animation, you can easily enlarge elements when a user hovers over them.

lessons.title: Flip Box
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to creating interactive 3D flip boxes using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure the container elements and apply transform properties to achieve both horizontal and vertical flipping effects upon mouse hover.

lessons.title: Center Vertically
lessons.summary: This lesson provides practical techniques for centering elements within a container using CSS. It covers both vertical and horizontal alignment methods, including the use of absolute positioning with transform properties and modern Flexbox layouts to achieve precise element placement.

lessons.title: Center Button in DIV
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to center a button within a container using CSS. It covers techniques for vertical and horizontal alignment, including the use of absolute positioning with transform properties and modern Flexbox layouts.

lessons.title: Center a List
lessons.summary: To center a list in CSS, wrap the `<ul>` element in a container `<div>` and apply `text-align: center` to the parent. Then, set the `<ul>` to `display: inline-block` to ensure it centers correctly while allowing you to left-align the list items for a cleaner appearance.

lessons.title: Transition on Hover
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement smooth visual changes in CSS by using the transition property. It demonstrates how to apply these effects to elements like buttons, allowing property values such as opacity and background color to animate gracefully when a user hovers over them.

lessons.title: Arrows
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create directional arrows using CSS by applying specific border styles and rotation transforms to inline elements. By combining a base class with directional modifiers, you can easily generate up, down, left, and right arrows for your web projects.

lessons.title: Shapes
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on utilizing CSS to design and implement various geometric shapes for web development. It focuses on leveraging styling properties to create visual elements directly through code, enhancing layout aesthetics without the need for external image assets.

lessons.title: Download Link
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to use the HTML `download` attribute to trigger file downloads when a user clicks a hyperlink. It covers how to implement the attribute with an `href` and demonstrates how to optionally rename the downloaded file by providing a specific value to the attribute.

lessons.title: Full Height Element
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to stretch HTML elements to match the full height of the browser window using CSS. By setting the height of the html, body, and target elements to 100%, you can ensure your content consistently occupies the entire vertical space of the viewport.

lessons.title: Browser Window
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on how to design a browser window interface using CSS. It includes interactive examples that demonstrate the necessary styling techniques to replicate a web browser's appearance for your projects.

lessons.title: Custom Scrollbar
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to style browser scrollbars using the non-standard `::-webkit-scrollbar` pseudo-elements. It provides practical CSS examples for customizing the track and handle, while noting that these features are primarily supported in Chrome, Safari, Edge, and Opera.

lessons.title: Hide Scrollbar
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to hide scrollbars using CSS. It covers using the `overflow` property to disable scrolling entirely, as well as browser-specific techniques to hide scrollbars while maintaining full page scroll functionality.

lessons.title: Show/Force Scrollbar
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to force the display of scrollbars on a webpage using the CSS `overflow` property. By applying `overflow: scroll` or specific directional properties like `overflow-y` and `overflow-x`, developers can ensure scrollbars remain visible regardless of content length.

lessons.title: Device Look
lessons.summary: This lesson provides practical guidance on using CSS to create realistic visual representations of smartphones, tablets, and laptops. Through interactive examples, learners discover how to style web elements to mimic the appearance of various digital devices.

lessons.title: Contenteditable Border
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to remove the default focus border that appears on elements with the `contenteditable` attribute. By applying the `outline: 0px solid transparent;` CSS property to the `[contenteditable]` selector, you can achieve a cleaner, borderless look for your editable content.

lessons.title: Placeholder Color
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to customize the color of input placeholder text using the CSS `::placeholder` pseudo-element. It also highlights the importance of setting `opacity: 1` to ensure consistent styling across different browsers, particularly Firefox.

lessons.title: Disable Resizing of Textarea
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to prevent users from manually resizing a textarea element on a webpage. By applying the CSS `resize: none;` property to the textarea selector, developers can effectively lock the element's dimensions for a consistent layout.

lessons.title: Disable Text Selection
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to prevent users from highlighting or selecting text on a webpage using the CSS `user-select` property. By applying the `none` value to specific elements, developers can effectively disable default browser selection behavior across different platforms.

lessons.title: Text Selection Color
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to customize the visual appearance of highlighted text on a webpage using the CSS `::selection` pseudo-element. By applying specific color and background properties to this selector, developers can override default browser styles to match their site's branding.

lessons.title: Bullet Color
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to customize the appearance of list items using CSS. By applying the `::marker` pseudo-element to `<li>` tags, users can easily modify the color of list bullets to better suit their design requirements.

lessons.title: Vertical Line
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a vertical line in web design using CSS. It provides code examples for creating a basic line with the border-left property and explains how to precisely center it on a page using absolute positioning and margin adjustments.

lessons.title: Dividers
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating custom visual dividers using HTML and CSS. By applying specific border styles—such as dashed, dotted, solid, and rounded—to the `<hr>` element, learners can effectively enhance the layout and organization of their web content.

lessons.title: Text Divider
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create responsive text dividers using CSS flexbox. By applying the `::before` and `::after` pseudo-elements to a container, you can effectively center text between two horizontal lines to enhance your webpage layout.

lessons.title: Animate Icons
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create animated icons by cycling through a sequence of Font Awesome characters using JavaScript. By utilizing the `setTimeout` and `setInterval` functions, developers can replace icons at timed intervals to simulate dynamic effects like a charging battery.

lessons.title: Countdown Timer
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a dynamic countdown timer using JavaScript. It demonstrates how to calculate the time difference between the current moment and a target date, updating the display every second using the setInterval method.

lessons.title: Typewriter
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to creating a dynamic typewriter effect using HTML and JavaScript. By utilizing the `setTimeout()` method, learners can programmatically append characters to a text element, effectively simulating real-time typing on a webpage.

lessons.title: Coming Soon Page
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building a professional "Coming Soon" page using HTML and CSS for layout and styling. It further explains how to enhance the page by integrating a functional JavaScript countdown timer to engage users until the launch date.

lessons.title: Chat Messages
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on designing chat message interfaces using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure message containers, style avatars, and use CSS properties to align text and images, enabling developers to create visually distinct, interactive chat layouts.

lessons.title: Popup Chat Window
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building a functional popup chat window using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the essential steps to structure the form, apply styling for a fixed-position interface, and implement basic toggle functionality to open and close the chat window.

lessons.title: Split Screen
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on creating a 50/50 split-screen layout using HTML and CSS. By utilizing fixed positioning and transform properties, you will learn how to divide the viewport into two distinct sections with perfectly centered content.

lessons.title: Testimonials
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building responsive testimonial sections for websites. It demonstrates how to structure the HTML and apply CSS styling, including media queries, to ensure that user feedback displays effectively across different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Section Counter
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide for building a responsive "section counter" to showcase business metrics. It demonstrates how to structure the layout using HTML and style the data cards with CSS, including media queries to ensure the design remains functional across different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Quotes Slideshow
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building an interactive quotes slideshow using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers the structural setup, styling for visual presentation, and the logic required to navigate between slides using buttons and indicators.

lessons.title: Closable List Items
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to create interactive list items that can be hidden by clicking a close button. By combining HTML structure, CSS styling, and JavaScript event listeners, learners can implement a functional UI component to remove specific list entries dynamically.

lessons.title: Typical Device Breakpoints
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement responsive design by using five standard CSS media query breakpoints. By categorizing devices into groups ranging from phones to large desktops, developers can efficiently manage layout adjustments across various screen sizes.

lessons.title: Draggable HTML Element
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to creating a draggable HTML element using CSS and JavaScript. It demonstrates how to implement mouse event listeners to track cursor movement and dynamically update an element's absolute position on the screen.

lessons.title: JS Media Queries
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to implement responsive design using the JavaScript `window.matchMedia()` method. It demonstrates how to create a `MediaQueryList` object and attach event listeners to trigger dynamic changes in your web page based on viewport size adjustments.

lessons.title: Syntax Highlighter
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on implementing a syntax highlighter to improve the readability of code snippets. It outlines the necessary HTML structure and demonstrates how to apply a JavaScript function to dynamically colorize and format code content within a web page.

lessons.title: JS Animations
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to create web animations by programmatically modifying element styles using JavaScript timers. It covers the necessary CSS positioning requirements and demonstrates how to use `setInterval` to execute gradual frame updates for smooth visual movement.

lessons.title: JS String Length
lessons.summary: In JavaScript, you can easily determine the number of characters in a string by using the built-in `length` property. This property returns the total count of characters, providing a simple and efficient way to measure string size within your code.

lessons.title: JS Exponentiation
lessons.summary: This lesson introduces the JavaScript exponentiation operator (**), which raises a base to a specified power, functioning similarly to the Math.pow() method. It also covers the exponentiation assignment operator (**=), allowing for concise updates to variable values through power calculations.

lessons.title: JS Default Parameters
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to handle missing arguments in JavaScript functions by assigning default values. It contrasts the traditional manual check for undefined parameters with the more efficient ES6 syntax, which allows developers to define default values directly within the function declaration.

lessons.title: JS Random Number
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to generate random integers within a specific range using JavaScript's `Math.random()` and `Math.floor()` methods. It provides two distinct functions to handle scenarios where the maximum value is either excluded or included in the result.

lessons.title: JS Sort Numeric Array
lessons.summary: To sort a numeric array in JavaScript, use the `sort()` method with a compare function. Providing `a - b` sorts the numbers in ascending order, while `b - a` sorts them in descending order. This approach ensures correct numerical sorting, which differs from the default string-based behavior.

lessons.title: JS Spread Operator
lessons.summary: The JavaScript spread operator (...) allows you to efficiently expand iterables like arrays and objects into individual elements. It is a powerful tool for merging data, copying structures, and extracting specific values, while also providing a concise way to handle property overrides in objects.

lessons.title: JS Scroll Into View
lessons.summary: This lesson introduces the `scrollIntoView()` method, a JavaScript function used to programmatically bring a specific element into the browser's visible area. It provides a concise code example demonstrating how to target an element and trigger the scroll action for improved user navigation.

lessons.title: Get Current Date
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to retrieve the current date in JavaScript using the `new Date()` constructor. It provides a concise code example and directs learners to further resources for mastering date manipulation.

lessons.title: Get Current URL
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to retrieve the current page's web address using the JavaScript `window.location.href` property. It provides a practical code example demonstrating how to display the full URL directly within an HTML element.

lessons.title: Get Current Screen Size
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to retrieve the current browser window dimensions using JavaScript. By utilizing the `window.innerWidth` and `window.innerHeight` properties, developers can easily access the page's height and width, excluding toolbars and scrollbars.

lessons.title: Get Iframe Elements
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to access and manipulate elements within an iframe using JavaScript. By utilizing the `contentWindow.document` property, developers can target specific elements inside an embedded document to perform actions like modifying CSS styles.

lessons.title: Create a Website
lessons.summary: W3Schools Spaces offers a free, browser-based platform for building and hosting websites using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The lesson guides beginners through account setup, template selection, and code editing, empowering users to create and publish their own projects with ease.

lessons.title: Make a Website
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a responsive website from scratch using HTML and CSS. It covers essential structural elements like headers, navigation bars, and flexible column layouts, while demonstrating how to use media queries to ensure compatibility across all devices.

lessons.title: Make a Static Website
lessons.summary: This lesson introduces static websites as a secure, cost-effective, and beginner-friendly way to build web content using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It guides learners through building from scratch or using templates via the W3Schools Spaces platform to gain practical web development experience.

lessons.title: Host a Static Website
lessons.summary: This lesson introduces W3Schools Spaces as a simplified platform for hosting static websites using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It guides users through creating an account, uploading files, and managing projects directly in the browser, while highlighting built-in HTTPS security features.

lessons.title: Make a Website (W3.CSS)
lessons.summary: This lesson guides beginners through building a responsive website using the W3.CSS framework. It covers the importance of layout planning, implementing semantic HTML5 elements, and utilizing CSS classes to create navigation bars, slideshows, and structured content sections.

lessons.title: Make a Website (BS3)
lessons.summary: This lesson provides an introduction to building responsive, mobile-first websites using the Bootstrap 3 CSS framework. It offers practical resources, including live demos and tutorials, to help beginners leverage this popular tool for efficient web development.

lessons.title: Make a Website (BS4)
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical introduction to building responsive, mobile-first websites using the Bootstrap 4 framework. It offers hands-on examples and resources to help learners effectively utilize this popular tool for modern web development.

lessons.title: Make a Website (BS5)
lessons.summary: This lesson introduces Bootstrap 5 as a powerful, free framework for building responsive, mobile-first websites. It provides practical examples and resources to help learners create professional web layouts efficiently. Users are encouraged to explore tutorials and templates to master the tool.

lessons.title: Create and View a Website
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a step-by-step guide to creating and viewing a basic HTML web page using a simple text editor. It covers writing code, saving files with the correct extensions, and opening them in a browser, while also introducing online tools for quick testing and web development.

lessons.title: Create a Link Tree Website
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a comprehensive guide to building a personalized link tree website using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers both manual development and the use of customizable templates, offering practical steps to structure, design, and host your site via W3Schools Spaces.

lessons.title: Create a Portfolio
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a comprehensive guide on building a professional online portfolio to showcase skills and attract career opportunities. It details essential sections like the hero, about, projects, and contact areas, while offering technical steps to develop the site using HTML, CSS, and JS.

lessons.title: Create a Resume
lessons.summary: This lesson guides you through creating a professional online resume using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It covers essential content sections, design planning, and technical implementation using W3Schools Spaces to help you build and publish a personalized digital portfolio for global accessibility.

lessons.title: Make a Restaurant Website
lessons.summary: This lesson explains the importance of a restaurant website for branding, online reservations, and customer engagement. It guides learners through the core technical requirements—HTML, CSS, and JavaScript—while outlining essential site sections and offering options to build from scratch or templates.

lessons.title: Make a Business Website
lessons.summary: This lesson explains the importance of a professional website for business credibility and lead generation. It outlines essential structural components, such as hero and contact sections, while providing guidance on using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript or ready-made templates to build your site.

lessons.title: Make a WebBook
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a foundational guide to creating responsive digital books using HTML and CSS. It covers essential document structure, the implementation of a table of contents, and the process of linking individual chapters to ensure a functional, multi-page reading experience.

lessons.title: Center Website
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to horizontally center a website using CSS by applying a container element with a defined `max-width` and the `margin: auto` property. This technique ensures the content remains centered on wider screens while adapting to smaller displays.

lessons.title: Contact Section
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a responsive contact section for web pages. It covers the necessary HTML structure and CSS styling, including media queries to ensure the layout adapts seamlessly to smaller screen sizes.

lessons.title: About Page
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a responsive "About Us" page using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure content with cards and columns, ensuring the layout adapts seamlessly to different screen sizes through effective styling and media queries.

lessons.title: Big Header
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on styling a website header using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure the header element and apply specific properties, such as padding, text alignment, background color, and font size, to create a visually appealing, large-scale header design.

lessons.title: Example Website
lessons.summary: This lesson introduces the fundamentals of building a responsive website using HTML and CSS to ensure compatibility across all devices. It provides a practical demo for testing layout adaptability and offers resources for creating custom websites through the W3Schools Spaces platform.

lessons.title: 2 Column Layout
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to build a two-column layout using CSS floats or the modern Flexbox approach. It covers techniques for creating equal and unequal column widths, as well as implementing responsive design to ensure layouts stack correctly on smaller screens.

lessons.title: 3 Column Layout
lessons.summary: This lesson explains how to build a three-column layout using CSS floats and HTML containers. It provides practical examples for creating equal, unequal, and responsive column structures that automatically stack on smaller screens, ensuring a flexible design for various device widths.

lessons.title: 4 Column Layout
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to build a four-column grid layout using CSS floats and HTML containers. It further explains how to implement responsive design by using media queries to stack columns vertically on smaller screens, ensuring optimal display across different devices.

lessons.title: Expanding Grid
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building an interactive expanding grid using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It demonstrates how to create a responsive layout where clicking grid items triggers the display of specific content containers, complete with a functional close button feature.

lessons.title: List Grid View
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to implement a toggleable list and grid view for web content. By combining HTML structure, CSS styling, and JavaScript functions, users can dynamically adjust the layout width of elements to switch between a single-column list and a multi-column grid display.

lessons.title: Mixed Column Layout
lessons.summary: This lesson teaches how to build a responsive mixed column layout using CSS. By utilizing media queries, you will learn to transition a grid from four columns to two columns, and finally to a full-width stacked layout as the screen size decreases.

lessons.title: Column Cards
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on building responsive column cards using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure card elements within a grid and apply styling, including media queries to ensure the layout adapts seamlessly to different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Zig Zag Layout
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a responsive zig-zag layout using CSS. By utilizing HTML containers and media queries, you can create alternating column structures that automatically stack for optimal viewing on smaller screens.

lessons.title: Blog Layout
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a responsive blog layout using HTML and CSS. It demonstrates how to structure content into columns and implement media queries to ensure the design adapts seamlessly across different screen sizes.

lessons.title: Google Charts
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide on integrating Google Charts into web pages. It demonstrates how to set up a container element, load the necessary Chart API, and use JavaScript to render a dynamic pie chart with custom data and display options.

lessons.title: Google Fonts
lessons.summary: This lesson introduces Google Fonts as a free, accessible resource for web typography. It explains how to integrate these fonts into your web pages, highlighting the extensive library of over 810 options available for developers to enhance their site's design.

lessons.title: Google Font Pairings
lessons.summary: This lesson emphasizes the importance of effective typography in design by showcasing a curated selection of popular Google Font pairings. It provides practical visual examples to help designers choose harmonious font combinations for their web projects.

lessons.title: Google Set up Analytics
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a comprehensive, step-by-step guide to setting up Google Analytics 4 for your website. It covers the entire process from account creation and property configuration to installing the Global Site Tag (gtag.js) and verifying data collection in real time.

lessons.title: Convert Weight
lessons.summary: This lesson teaches how to build a functional weight converter using HTML and JavaScript. It provides practical code examples for real-time input processing and includes comprehensive conversion formulas for units such as pounds, kilograms, ounces, grams, and stones.

lessons.title: Convert Temperature
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a functional temperature converter using HTML and JavaScript. It explains the necessary code implementation and outlines the mathematical formulas required to convert values between Fahrenheit, Celsius, and Kelvin scales.

lessons.title: Convert Length
lessons.summary: This lesson demonstrates how to build a functional length converter using HTML and JavaScript. It provides practical code examples for input handling and includes comprehensive reference tables detailing the mathematical formulas required to convert between various units of measurement.

lessons.title: Convert Speed
lessons.summary: This lesson provides a practical guide to building a speed converter using HTML and JavaScript. It explains how to implement input elements and event handlers, while offering a comprehensive reference table of mathematical formulas for converting between MPH, KPH, Knots, and Mach.

lessons.title: Get a Developer Job
lessons.summary: This guide outlines a practical roadmap for aspiring developers to land their first job through consistent practice, project building, and networking. It emphasizes mastering core web technologies, creating a strong portfolio, and leveraging free resources to gain essential industry experience.

lessons.title: Become a Front-End Dev.
lessons.summary: This guide introduces the role of a Front-End Developer, focusing on the essential mastery of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It outlines a clear learning path, including key frameworks and additional skills, to help beginners build interactive web interfaces and prepare for a career in the field.

lessons.title: Hire Developers
lessons.summary: This guide provides a practical checklist for hiring developers, emphasizing the importance of defining specific technical needs and building a targeted candidate pipeline. It highlights the value of personalized outreach and balanced evaluation, ensuring both technical proficiency and cultural fit.
